rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes; use implementation annexes for bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE expressions are visible in the current scope
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without proper exposure
Preserve the original AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes
